microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: The Microsoft 365 Copilot SKU must be discovered per tenant with GET /subscribedSkus; the tenant skuId, skuPartNumber, prepaidUnits, and consumedUnits are read from that response rather than hardcoded.

In the Microsoft licensing reference the string ID Microsoft_365_Copilot identifies the current Microsoft 365 Copilot product; some tenants present the legacy string ID M365_Copilot. Product display name, skuPartNumber, and skuId are distinct values.

LicenseAssignment.ReadWrite.All is the least-privileged Microsoft Graph permission for both group assignLicense (POST /groups/{id}/assignLicense) and user assignLicense (POST /users/{id}/assignLicense); usageLocation must be set before assignment and removeLicenses accepts skuId GUIDs.

LicenseAssignment.Read.All is the least-privileged Microsoft Graph permission for read-only subscribed-SKU and license discovery, enabling capture of the tenant skuId and current assignment state before any change.

Group-based licensing reports assignment errors such as insufficient licenses, conflicting service plans, missing dependencies, and usage-location problems, does not process nested groups, and inherited licenses cannot be removed by a direct user removeLicenses call.

The Copilot Control System in the Microsoft 365 admin center manages Copilot license assignment, agents, usage reporting, and licensing recommendations; app and feature behaviors are governed separately by Cloud Policy, so this solution complements rather than replaces those built-in controls.

licenses: Microsoft 365 Copilot licenses available in the tenant for a disposable test cohort; none are assigned during this read-only cycle
Qualifying Microsoft 365 base license (E3/E5, Business Standard/Premium, or Office 365 E3/E5) for the representative test cohort
roles: Global Reader for read-only directory, subscribed-SKU, and assignment inspection
Microsoft Graph LicenseAssignment.Read.All delegated permission for read-only SKU and assignment discovery
features: Microsoft 365 Copilot provisioned in the tenant
Microsoft Entra usage location populated for the representative test cohort
capacityRequirements: One disposable test cohort group with representative sample members for read-only metadata inspection
dataRequirements: Representative sample records only; no user PII persisted to committed lab evidence
prerequisites.notes: First lab cycle is read-only and detect-only. No license assignment, removal, or group membership change is performed. Disposable license assignment is deferred until exact prior-state capture and ownership can be enforced for a safe, reversible mutation.

phases.title: Confirm tenant identity and read-only preconditions

steps.intent: Confirm the approved lab tenant before performing any read so the discovery runs against the expected organization.
steps.expected: The organization identity returned matches the approved lab tenant.
steps.operatorNote: Compare the returned id and displayName with the separately maintained sanctioned-lab authorization record. Do not proceed on any mismatch.

readBack.expectation: The returned organization identity matches the separately maintained sanctioned-lab authorization record and is recorded before any further read.

phases.title: Read-only Copilot SKU, cohort, and assignment discovery

steps.intent: Discover the Microsoft 365 Copilot SKU from the tenant catalog and capture its tenant skuId and seat counts.
steps.expected: A subscribedSku with skuPartNumber Microsoft_365_Copilot (or legacy M365_Copilot) is present and its tenant skuId and seat counts are captured.

api.requestTemplate: GET /subscribedSkus - match skuPartNumber 'Microsoft_365_Copilot' (legacy 'M365_Copilot'); capture the tenant skuId, prepaidUnits.enabled and consumedUnits. Do not hardcode a SKU GUID.

readBack.expectation: The Copilot subscribedSku is located by skuPartNumber and its tenant skuId and available-seat count (prepaidUnits.enabled minus consumedUnits) are captured.

steps.intent: Read the disposable test cohort group metadata and any existing license assignments without modifying them.
steps.expected: The test cohort group metadata and any existing group assignedLicenses are read without modification.
steps.command: $cohortGroupId = $env:RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cohortGroupId)) { throw 'RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID is required.' }; try { [void]([guid]$cohortGroupId) } catch { throw 'RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID must be a GUID.' }; $uri = 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups/{0}?$select=id,displayName,assignedLicenses,membershipRule' -f $cohortGroupId; Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri $uri

readBack.expectation: The test cohort group metadata and any current group-level assignedLicenses are captured read-only.

steps.intent: Record per-user usage location and whether Copilot is directly assigned or inherited from a group for the representative cohort.
steps.expected: Current per-user assignment state (direct vs inherited) and usageLocation are captured read-only.
steps.command: $cohortGroupId = $env:RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cohortGroupId)) { throw 'RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID is required.' }; try { [void]([guid]$cohortGroupId) } catch { throw 'RTR_LAB_COHORT_GROUP_ID must be a GUID.' }; $uri = 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups/{0}/members/microsoft.graph.user?$select=id,usageLocation,assignedLicenses,licenseAssignmentStates' -f $cohortGroupId; Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri $uri

readBack.expectation: For each representative user, prior assignment state (direct vs inherited) and usageLocation are captured to support safe future rollback decisions.

steps.intent: Generate a dry-run wave manifest to confirm the solution stages cohorts without assigning any license.
steps.expected: The manifest records licenseAssignment.mode 'preview' and cohort assignmentState 'manifest-only'; no license is assigned.

readBack.expectation: licenseAssignment.mode is 'preview', skuIdSource is 'tenant-subscribedSkus-discovery', and no SKU GUID appears in the manifest.

phases.title: Verify no seat change and confirm source currency

steps.intent: Re-read the Copilot SKU seat counts as corroborating evidence that the read-only cycle did not consume a license seat.
steps.expected: consumedUnits for the Copilot SKU is compared with the earlier capture; any difference is investigated as possible concurrent tenant activity rather than attributed automatically to this read-only run.

api.requestTemplate: GET /subscribedSkus - compare consumedUnits for the Copilot SKU against the value captured during exercise.

readBack.expectation: The comparison records whether consumedUnits changed and notes that the preview-only manifest and absence of write calls are the authoritative no-mutation evidence.

steps.intent: Confirm each cited Microsoft Learn source remains published and matches the recorded lifecycle state.
steps.expected: All cited Microsoft Learn sources resolve and remain generally available.
steps.operatorNote: Open each microsoftSourceClaims.sourceUrl and confirm the Microsoft Learn page is still published and its lifecycle state remains generally-available.

readBack.expectation: Every cited Microsoft Learn source resolves and its lifecycle state still matches the contract.

steps.intent: Attest that no mutable change occurred so cleanup is not required for this read-only cycle.
steps.expected: Operator note confirms mutations were empty and no cleanup is required.
steps.operatorNote: Attest that mutations were empty: no license assignment, no license removal, and no group membership change occurred during this read-only cycle.

readBack.expectation: Operator attests no mutable change occurred and no cleanup is required.

requiredArtifacts.description: Redacted organization id/displayName response plus reference to the separately maintained sanctioned-lab authorization record.
Redacted /subscribedSkus read showing the Copilot skuPartNumber, tenant skuId, and prepaidUnits/consumedUnits used to confirm seat availability.
Test cohort group metadata and per-user direct-vs-inherited assignment state and usageLocation, with user identifiers redacted.
Dry-run wave manifest showing licenseAssignment.mode 'preview' and skuIdSource 'tenant-subscribedSkus-discovery' with no SKU GUID.
List of verified Microsoft Learn source URLs and lifecycle states supporting the reviewed rollout, licensing, and permission claims.
